rationale: Contract change, not test relaxation. The verifier behavior change (raise VerifierOutputParseError → logger.warning) is the entire point of ENG-150: a nonzero exit code paired with a reward file written DURING this run (after _clear_reward_outputs) should be accepted as a legitimate failure result, not treated as verifier_errored. The deleted test test_nonzero_test_script_cannot_turn_reward_file_into_pass was correctly removed because it asserts the OLD contract; the new class TestVerifierNonzeroExitRewardAcceptance adds 5 new test cases (classify_result outcomes, end-to-end Verifier.verify acceptance for both reward.txt and reward.json paths, plus a negative regression test ensuring rc!=0 with NO reward file still errors). The detector flagged this as test-relaxation because it saw a deleted test block without an immediately-adjacent compensating test in the same hunk, but the compensating coverage lives in a separate test file (test_oracle_chokepoint.py) — a real human reviewer would call this a legitimate contract migration.
